steps.title: Use the definition of mean
steps.content:
The mean of a list is
$$\frac{\text{sum of the values}}{\text{number of values}}$$
There are $5$ numbers, so the mean is automatically $\frac{1}{5}$ of the sum.

steps.title: Confirm with the actual sum
steps.content:
The sum is $2+10+3+7+6=28$, and the mean is $\frac{28}{5}$. As a fraction of the sum,
$$\frac{28/5}{28}=\frac{1}{5}=0.2$$
Accepted entries include 1/5, 0.2, or .2.
